purpose: Decompose Phase 7 (NPCs, Shops, Boss) into 7.A.1 / 7.A.2 / 7.B sub-phases mirroring the established X.A.1/X.A.2/X.B pattern from Phases 3 / 4 / 5 / 6. Phase 6 was end-to-end verified by phase-6.B (CI run 25331568922 green: deploy + 60 cross-runtime Playwright assertions + cross-OS atlas matrix on the new 1368-byte binary); Phase 7 is unblocked.
trigger: Phase 6.B was approved at HEAD of commit cc63bdf on 2026-05-04 with all gates green (deploy workflow success in 2m 40s; 20 e2e tests × 3 browsers = 60 assertions; cross-OS matrix byte-identical at the new ATLAS_DIGEST 35069834850591c6b72c1946629129a04ed2f1b9446de5ccdd75b28fe6005a47 across ubuntu-latest/macos-latest/windows-latest). The next unapproved phase per docs/PHASES.md is Phase 7 (NPCs, Shops, Boss). Phase 7 is comparable in surface area to Phase 6 — substantial new sim work (NPC + shop + boss-FSM mechanics, new action-vocabulary types for trade interactions), substantial new content (~3 NPC types + boss + 3 carry-forward Phase 3 item recipes = ~7 new atlas recipes), and new UI (shop interaction screens + win screen). The X.A.1/X.A.2/X.B decomposition that Phases 3 / 4 / 5 / 6 used is the right structural fit. Phase 7 is NOT a planning-gate phase per docs/PHASES.md:7 (planning gates are 1.A, 2, 3, 4, 8 only), so the decomposition is recorded in this phase-update artifact rather than a decision memo. Material design choices (NPC representation, shop transaction model, boss FSM, deterministic stock generation) are resolved during Phase 7.A.1's drift sweep via a brief strategy-planner pass if multiple plausible options surface; the output lives in the ARCHITECTURE.md Phase 7 section.

phase_6_b_post_approval_summary.jobs_all_green: build & test (1m 52s including 60 cross-runtime Playwright assertions); cross-os atlas equality (ubuntu-latest 23s + macos-latest 17s + windows-latest 2m 9s + aggregate 7s, all byte-identical at 35069834850591c6b72c1946629129a04ed2f1b9446de5ccdd75b28fe6005a47); deploy to GitHub Pages 10s
phase_6_b_post_approval_summary.verdict: Phase 6 (Items, Currency, Equipment) is complete and end-to-end verified. The deterministic-encoder discipline holds against the bumped 1368-byte atlas across all three OSes; the SIM_DIGEST is preserved at 321c09e5… (Phase 3 frozen contract); the new INVENTORY_DIGEST 9126830d… is pinned; the inventory-from-log reconstruction invariant is asserted; the long-deferred Phase 2 decision-memo decision 6 registry-immutability test landed alongside the 5→20 item registry expansion. No phase-update fix-up required.

phase_7_split_decision.rationale: Phase 7 introduces NPCs + shops + boss as net-new sim mechanics, ~7 new atlas recipes (3 NPCs + boss + 3 carry-forward Phase 3 items missing recipes from N4), ~2-4 new action-vocabulary types (shop interactions; the exact count depends on the chosen transaction model), boss FSM with deterministic phase transitions, and a win screen UI. This crosses sim, atlas, and ui layers and bumps the ATLAS_DIGEST + preset-seed expectedHash goldens. The X.A.1/X.A.2/X.B decomposition is the right structural fit and the established pattern. Phase 7 is NOT a planning-gate phase per docs/PHASES.md:7, so the decomposition is recorded here rather than via a decision-memo + architecture-red-team cycle. Material design choices are resolved during Phase 7.A.1's drift sweep via a brief strategy-planner pass if needed.

phase_7_A_1.name: drift-detection sweep
phase_7_A_1.scope: Address Phase 6.A.2 cosmetic carry-forward N4 — the 3 Phase 3 items that lack atlas recipes (item.stim-patch, item.trauma-pack, item.cyberdeck-mod-1). These are a latent renderer-throw if those items are ever placed on a floor; Phase 7's shop NPCs may stock them, so the recipes land in 7.A.1 to remove the latent risk before 7.A.2's NPC content goes live. Atlas regenerates with the 3 new sprites; ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values bump accordingly. Existing 23 sprite coordinates remain byte-identical per addendum 3a coordinate-stability. (This is the FIRST X.A.1 in the project's history that includes an atlas regeneration — typically X.A.1 is no-net-new-binary; the rationale is that the missing recipes are a code-review-flagged latent defect, and addressing them in 7.A.1 is the cleanest scope-isolation. The 3 new recipes are tightly bounded; atlas regeneration is well-tested by Phase 4-6.) Phase 6.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards N1/N2/N3/N5 (inventory count tie-break test, inventoryRemove validation throw test, inventory-replay deeper mutation, per-domain index comment) addressed where they touch files Phase 7.A.2 will modify. Update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md with Phase 7 frozen contracts: NPC data shape (deterministic ordering, FloorState.npcs collection or analogous); shop interaction action vocabulary additions with byte-explicit wire format per Phase 1 frozen contract; deterministic shop-transaction resolution path through rollBytes subhash with new domain anchors (shop:stock, shop:price, etc.); boss FSM contract with phase transitions and per-phase atk/def/hp scaling; boss-room spawn override (floor 10 already has bossArena from Phase 2; 7.A.2 wires the actual boss spawn); win-state transition (Phase 3 already has outcome=='won' detection on floor-10 boss-dead; Phase 7 extends with the win-screen UI activation contract). NO net-new NPC / shop / boss-FSM code lands in 7.A.1.
phase_7_A_1.expected_diff_size: small-to-medium — primarily docs/ARCHITECTURE.md additions + the 3 atlas recipe modules + atlas regeneration + ATLAS_DIGEST/preset-seed bumps + tests for the 3 new recipes. The atlas regen is an exception to the typical X.A.1 'no-binary-changes' rule, justified by the latent-defect carry-forward.

phase_7_A_2.name: sandbox-verifiable implementation
phase_7_A_2.scope: NPC + boss types in src/sim/types.ts (with deterministic ordering for NPC iteration); src/registries/npcs.ts with ~3 NPC types (ripperdoc, fixer, info-broker — categories pinned alphabetically); 2-4 new Action descriptors in src/core/encode.ts additive to the Phase 1 schema (talk/buy/sell or analogous; reuse existing TAG_TARGET=0x10 for npc-id and TAG_ITEM=0x20 for item-kind; no ACTION_VERSION bump); tick() extended in src/sim/turn.ts to handle NPC interactions with deterministic shop transactions (rollBytes with new domain anchors like shop:stock, shop:price); src/registries/atlas-recipes.ts adds ~4 new entries (3 NPCs already have one — npc.ripperdoc — so add fixer + info-broker; boss); boss FSM in src/sim/turn.ts (multi-phase state machine with deterministic transitions; phase tracked in Monster.aiState extended union or new field); boss-room spawn at floor 10 in src/sim/run.ts spawnFloorEntities; win-screen UI in new src/ui/win-screen.ts (reads RunState.outcome === 'won' and RunState.fingerprintInputs read-only per Phase 5 contract; renders shareable fingerprint widget); shop-interaction UI in new src/ui/shop.ts (read-only on RunState; shows NPC dialog + offered items + prices); assets/atlas.png regenerated with 3 NPCs + boss + (already-landed-in-7.A.1) 3 carry-forward item recipes = ~30 sprites total in atlas; ATLAS_DIGEST golden bumped + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values bumped; new BOSS_DIGEST or WIN_DIGEST golden — pin a fingerprint that buys → descends → defeats boss → wins; new self-test exercising the win loop; win-state reachability test asserting replay produces byte-identical final state hash.
phase_7_A_2.expected_diff_size: large — comparable to Phase 6.A.2 surface area (new sim mechanics + atlas regeneration + multiple golden bumps + new UI screens + extended action vocabulary + boss FSM). Bundle should grow ~3-5 KB raw / ~1-2 KB gzipped, staying under the 75 KB budget.

phase_7_B.name: live-deploy + cross-runtime + cross-OS verification
phase_7_B.scope: Verify the live GitHub Pages URL serves the full playable game with NPCs + shops + boss + win screen; verify cross-runtime Playwright on chromium/firefox/webkit exercises the buy → descend → defeat-boss scripted sequence on a fixed seed and asserts the final state hash matches a pinned golden; verify the Phase 4.B cross-os-atlas-equality matrix re-runs against the new atlas binary and reports green on all three OSes (the new atlas adds ~7 sprites — 3 NPCs + boss + 3 Phase 3 carry-forward items — but the deterministic-encoder discipline should hold; drift on macOS or Windows is a Phase 7.B blocker requiring a phase-update). Phase 7 is complete when 7.A.1, 7.A.2, and 7.B are all approved.
phase_7_B.expected_diff_size: minimal — observational verification + maybe one new Playwright test extension. The X.B verification commit's CI assertions only fire on the next push of the X.B commit itself per the established Phase 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B/5.B/6.B chicken-and-egg pattern.

phase_7_split_decision.atlas_regeneration_note: Phase 7.A.1's atlas regeneration is unusual — typically X.A.1 is no-binary-changes — but the 3 missing atlas recipes are a code-review-flagged latent defect (Phase 6.A.2 N4: renderer would throw if those items are ever placed on a floor). Addressing them in 7.A.1 ahead of 7.A.2's NPC content removes the latent risk and tightens scope isolation: 7.A.1 closes a defect, 7.A.2 lands the new feature. The atlas binary in 7.A.1 is intermediate; 7.A.2 will regenerate again with the NPC + boss recipes appended. Both regenerations preserve the existing sprite coordinates per the addendum 3a invariant.
phase_7_split_decision.docs_changes: docs/PHASES.md updated with the Phase 7 split callout block (mirroring the Phase 3 / 4 / 5 / 6 callouts) and the partitioned acceptance criteria for 7.A.1 / 7.A.2 / 7.B.

next_phase: Phase 7.A.1 — drift-detection sweep per the acceptance criteria recorded in docs/PHASES.md. The drift sweep should land: (1) atlas recipes for the 3 missing Phase 3 items (item.stim-patch, item.trauma-pack, item.cyberdeck-mod-1) — addresses Phase 6.A.2 code-review N4 latent-throw risk; (2) atlas regeneration → bumped ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values; (3) docs/ARCHITECTURE.md Phase 7 frozen-contract additions (NPC shape, shop action vocabulary, boss FSM contract, win-state transition); (4) Phase 6.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards N1/N2/N3/N5 if naturally touched. NO net-new NPC / shop / boss-FSM code in 7.A.1. Material design choices (NPC representation, shop transaction model, boss FSM, deterministic stock generation) may benefit from a brief strategy-planner pass if multiple plausible options surface. The next iteration of the agent loop should pick up phase-7.A.1 from the docs/PHASES.md acceptance criteria.
